content.full_text:
Fern Polrrrcel PRACTTcEs C ouurssroN
428 J Street o Suite 620 r Sacramento, CA g5gl1-232g
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
September 28,2007
Nicholas Gonzales Yuen
23lI A Russell Street
Berkeley, California 94705
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-07-150
Dear Mr. Yuen:
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the gift and income
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").' fnii advice is frsed on the facts
presented. The Commission does not act as a finder of fact when it renders advice. (ln re
Oglesby (1975) I FPPC Ops. 7t; Section 83114.)
QUESTTON
Are gift certificates you and your wife received from a company as a result of
business referrals made for solar panel installations considered either gift. o. income
under the Act?
CONCLUSION
The gift certificates are not gifts under the Act but are income and must be
reported on your statement of economic interests (Form 700) if the amount received is
$500 or more in value, as discussed below.
FACTS
You are an elected official in Alameda County, serving on the peralta Community
College Board of Trustees. Two year ago, you had solar panels installed on your house
by a company called NextEnergy. Pleased with the work NextEnergy performed, you
and your wife referred acquaintances to the company when they weie in the market for
1 Th"PoliticalReformActiscontainedinGovernmenrCodeSectionsgl000throughgl0l4. 
All
statutory references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations-of the Fair
PoliticalPractices Commission are contained in Sections l8ll0 through lgggT of Title 2 of the California
Code of Regulations. All regulatory references are to Title 2, Division-6 of the California Code of
Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.
q'4


File No. A-07-150
Page No. 2
solar panels. In addition, the New York Times interviewed you for a story about home
solar power that included a picture of you and your wife with your home. In the article,
you mentioned NextEnergy as the company that performed the work on your home. As a
result of the publicity, NextEnergy received several referrals in SouthernCalifomia,
opening a new market for them.
NextEnergy has a referral program2 whereby they give gift certificates for Trader
Joe's to customers who make referrals that result in new busineis. As a result of the
referrals you made, you and your wife received two gift certificates, each worth $250.
In our telephone conversation on September 27,2Cf7, you stated that you were
designated as a category one filer in your agency's conflict of interest code, and are
required to report gifts and income received from any source.
section 81002 (c) provide-s -",::r.Il'lno ,n.o-e of public officials rhat may
be materially affected by their official actions should be discloseA. fo fulfill that
purpose' the Act establishes requirements for certain public officials to file annual
statements of economic interest, disclosing their financial interests. (Section g72OO,
87300, and 87302.) Category one filers are required to report gifts and income received
from any source. Section 87302(b) states that when income isiequired to be disclosed,
the statement shall contain the same information required under Slction g7207 (a):
"(l) The name and address of each source of income aggregating
five hundred dollars ($5OO; or more in value, or fifty dollars ($iO)-o, *L."
in value if the income was a gift, and a general desciiption of the business
activity, if any, of each source."
In addition, the Act prohibits certain specified public officials from accepting gifts
totaling more than $390 from any single source in a calendar year. As a member of the
Peralta Community College Board of Trustees, you are subject to these provisions.
Subject to certain exceptions, the Act defines "gift" as "any payment that confers
a personal benefit on the recipient, to the extent that consideratiorroi equal or greater
value is not received and includes a rebate or discount in the price of anlrthingif value
unless the rebate or discount is made in the regular course of tusiness to *"rib.rs of the
public without regard to official status." (Section gZOZg.)
2 We note that, according to the company's website regarding its referral policy, ..[i]f the person
you are referring installs a N-extF,nergy solar system, you will receivei $250 gift c..tincute io irader Joe's
You may submit as many refenals as you wish." This policy applies to anyone who makes a qualifying
referral even if the referral is made by someone who is not r"uiio*"., according ,o u .o*puny
representative.


File No. A-07-150
Page No. 3
"fncome" is defined under Section g2030 (a) as:
"(a) ... a payment received, including but not limited to any salary, wage,
advance, dividend, interest, rent, proceeds from any sale, gift, incruding
any gift of food or beverage, roan, forgiveness or payment of indebtedness
received by the filer, reimbursement for expenser, p., diem, or
contribution to an insurance or pension progru- puia uy any person other
than an employer, and incruding any community prop"rty interest in the
income of a spouse."
In order to respond to your question, we must first determine whether the receipt
of the gift certificates qualifies a "gift" or as "income." Generally, this determination
hinges on whether or not "consideiation of equal or greater value is received,, in retum
for the payment.
The Act does not specifically address referral fees. However, Regulation 1g703.3
provides, with respect to real estate agents, that "commission income,, includes .,a
finder's or other referral fee" for refening a party to u trun.a"tion. In the NordAdvice
Letter, 4-96-131' we. applied this principle to a iublic orii"iur who received a finder,s fee
for "linking up a business with a financing lnstitutioJ'ror private financing, and
determined that this was the equivalent of "commission income.,, In the WienerAdvice
Letter, No' I-04-10'7, we also found that a "finder's r""" fuia to a public official, who was
not licensed as a real estate lrok9r or agent, for bringing u tuy". and seller together for a
real estate transaction was also "incomJ' for purposJs o? trr. Act, .,if consideration of
equal or greater value is provided in exchange for the payment.,,
Under the facts you have presented, it appears that it is an established business
practice of NextEnergy to provide gift certificui"r to p"rron, who make referrals that
result in business to the-company. Under these facts, the certificates u." p.ouid"d in
consideration for the referrals made and are, therefore, ..irrcome,, under the Act.
You have indicated that the two $250 certificates were each issued jointly to you
and your wife'3 Under these facts, your reportable income is equal to your community
property share of the total income, which ii $ZSO (($ZSO X Z) + 2). Because this amount
does not meet the $500 threshold under the Act ro,,"fortirg ir"o*", you are not
required to report this income on your statement of ecbnomic interests. [f, however, you
should make additional referrals and receive further referral fees, or other income, from
' The determination as to what amount is reportable by you it strictly based on in whose name the
certificates were actually issued. If one certificate was issued to you and one to your wite, for example,
you would have a different reportable amount than that specified above. Commission advice is based on
the facts presented. The commission does not act as a finder of fact. (rn re ogresb1,, supra.)


File No. A-07-150
Page No. 4
NextEnergy aggregating to $500 or more during the reporting period, the full amount
received must be reported.a
If you have other questions on this matter, prease contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
By: William
Senior Counsel, Legal Division
wJLjgl
4 In that case, you would also have a source of income economic interest in NextEnergy and may
be precluded from participating in any governmental decisions in which they were involved.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Are gift certificates you and your wife received from a company as a result of
business referrals made for solar panel installations considered either gift. o. income
under the Act?

CONCLUSION: The gift certificates are not gifts under the Act but are income and must be
reported on your statement of economic interests (Form 700) if the amount received is
$500 or more in value, as discussed below.
